prerequisites: An Account API Token, found on the API Tokens page in the Account section. It is accessible only to the Account Owner and Account Admins, and authorizes account-level actions such as creating Servers and adding Sender Signatures or Domains.

A Server API Token, found on the API Tokens tab of an individual Server. It authorizes server-level actions such as sending messages, querying sent messages, and using the Bounce API. A Server can have up to 3 API Tokens.
Network access to the Postmark API base URL https://api.postmarkapp.com over HTTPS.

authMethods.name: Account API Token (X-Postmark-Account-Token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticates account-level API requests by sending the Account API Token in the X-Postmark-Account-Token HTTP header. Restricted to the Account Owner and Account Admins. Maps to the X-Postmark-Account-Token secret field.

authMethods.name: Server API Token (X-Postmark-Server-Token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticates server-level API requests by sending a Server's API Token in the X-Postmark-Server-Token HTTP header. Available to Account Owners, Account Admins, and that Server's Server Admins. Maps to the X-Postmark-Server-Token secret field.

setupSteps.title: Get your Account API Token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Postmark and open the API Tokens page in the Account section. Copy the Account API Token. Only the Account Owner and Account Admins can view this token. It is used for account-level actions such as creating Servers and managing Sender Signatures or Domains.

setupSteps.title: Get a Server API Token
setupSteps.body: Open the Server you want to read from and go to its API Tokens tab. Copy a Server API Token (a Server can hold up to 3). This token authorizes server-level actions such as listing sent messages, bounces, and other per-server data.

setupSteps.title: Provide both tokens to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the Account API Token in the X-Postmark-Account-Token field and the Server API Token in the X-Postmark-Server-Token field. Both fields are required. Postmark sends these as HTTP headers to https://api.postmarkapp.com; a missing or invalid token returns HTTP 401 (Unauthorized).

sources.title: What are the Account and Server API Tokens, and SMTP Tokens? - Postmark Support
Overview - Postmark Developer Documentation (API authentication and base URL)
